Without assetmapper.Options, assets use compiled construction and bind both
*assetmapper.Compiled and assetmapper.Server.
-- go.mod --
module app

go 1.26

require github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/assetmapper v0.0.0

replace github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/assetmapper => ASSETSDIR
-- main.go --
package main

import "os"

func main() { os.Exit(run()) }
-- web/assets.go --
package web

import "embed"

//fabrik:assets
//go:embed all:assets
var Assets embed.FS
-- web/assets/app.css --
body {}
-- web/web.go --
package web

import (
	"net/http"

	"github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/assetmapper"
)

type Handlers struct {
	Assets assetmapper.Server
}

//fabrik:http GET /{$}
func (h *Handlers) Home(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {}
-- want/main.gen.go --
// Code generated by fabrik. DO NOT EDIT.
// Regenerate with: fabrik wire

package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"

	"github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/assetmapper"
	"github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/router"

	"app/web"
)

func run() int {
	if err := func() error {
		// Providers
		assetCompiled, err := assetmapper.Build([]assetmapper.Root{
			{FS: web.Assets, Dir: "assets"},
		}, nil)
		if err != nil {
			return err
		}
		webHandlers := &web.Handlers{
			Assets: assetCompiled,
		}

		r := router.New()

		// Register
		r.Handle("/assets/", assetCompiled.Handler())
		r.Method("GET", "/{$}", webHandlers.Home)
		return nil
	}(); err != nil {
		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "app:", err)
		return 1
	}
	return 0
}
